items.title: Supervised learning
items.description: Supervised learning requires human-provided labels for each training example. Self-supervised learning derives pseudo-labels from data structure and typically pretrains representations before any human labels are used for fine-tuning.

items.title: Unsupervised learning
items.description: Both use unlabeled raw data, but self-supervised learning always frames a prediction loss on pseudo-labels (pretext tasks). Classic unsupervised methods like k-means or PCA discover structure without an explicit surrogate classification objective.

items.title: Embeddings & representation
items.description: The primary deliverable of SSL pretraining is useful embeddings — dense vector representations of text, images, or multimodal inputs that downstream tasks consume via fine-tuning, retrieval, or prompting.

items.title: Generative AI
items.description: Large language models and multimodal foundation models depend on self-supervised pretraining (next-token prediction, masked LM, contrastive image–text training) at scale before instruction tuning and deployment as generative systems.
